title: T-008 — API: Items list (GET /api/technical/items) with filters
prompt:
# T-008 — API: Items list (GET /api/technical/items) with filters

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §6.2, §6.5
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Items list endpoint with filters, pagination, RLS-aware SELECT.

Use Server Action variant via app/api/technical/items/route.ts. Pagination via cursor or offset+limit (consistent with project convention). Filter by allergens uses EXISTS subquery joining item_allergen_profiles. Response shape: { items: ItemDTO[], total, next_cursor }. ItemDTO maps SQL columns to camelCase.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- app/api/technical/items/route.ts [create]
- lib/technical/items/queries.ts [create]
- lib/technical/items/schemas.ts [create]
- tests/api/items-list.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- Item create (T-009)
- Item detail UI
- D365 pull

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given org A and org B both have items, when GET /api/technical/items is called as org A, then only org A items are returned (RLS).
2. Given query type=fg&status=active, when records exist, then only fg+active items are returned.
3. Given query allergens=A01, when an item profile has A01, then it appears; otherwise it does not.
4. Given missing/invalid auth, when GET runs, then 401 is returned.

## Test strategy
- RED test asserting RLS isolation + filter combos.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/api/items-list.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not return private_jsonb to non-owner roles.
- Do not bypass RLS via service-role connection.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
